steps.title: Read the sentence and identify what comes before and after the blank
steps.content:
The text reads: "Cree director Chantelle Marie Anderson joined the growing number of Indigenous women adding their voices to North American ______ film, a selection of the Native Women in Film Festival, is about a woman exploring her Métis identity." Before the blank is a complete independent clause ending at "cinema." After the blank begins a new independent clause starting with "film… is about." Two independent clauses cannot be joined without a period, semicolon, or comma + coordinating conjunction.
  • Choice A — cinema her: Places "her" directly after "cinema" with no punctuation, producing a run-on sentence with no separator between two independent clauses. ✗
  • Choice B — cinema and her: Uses a coordinating conjunction but no comma before it, creating a comma splice error; "and her" also fails grammatically here. ✗
  • Choice C — cinema. Her: Places a period after "cinema," correctly ending the first independent clause, then starts the second with "Her" as a new sentence. ✓
  • Choice D — cinema, her: A comma alone cannot join two independent clauses; this creates a comma splice. ✗

steps.title: Confirm by testing the full sentence with each option
steps.content: Only the correct answer produces two properly separated, grammatically complete sentences. The other choices all attempt to join two independent clauses without a legal boundary marker. That is the correct answer.
